symptoms.symptom_name: Ischaemic rest pain in the toes relieved by dependency

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rest pain relieved by dependency is characteristic of critical limb ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Progression from exertional claudication to rest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The transition from claudication to rest pain marks progression to critical ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Cool, pale, shiny, hairless foot with hypersensitivity (trophic ischaemic changes)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic trophic changes reflect prolonged severe hypoperfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: No sudden cold/mottled/numb/paralysed foot (no acute limb ischaemia at present)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of the 6 Ps indicates critical (not yet acute) limb ischaemia, allowing urgent same-day rather than immediate-minutes escalation - but it must be safety-netted.

symptoms.symptom_name: Current heavy smoker, not on antiplatelet (very high vascular risk)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Heavy smoking drives the atherosclerosis and acutely vasoconstricts, worsening perfusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absent distal pulses, positive Buerger's test (elevation pallor/dependent rubor), delayed capillary refill; ABI severely reduced

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absent pulses with elevation pallor/dependent rubor and a severely reduced ABI confirm critical limb ischaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Severely reduced ABI and stenoses/occlusions on vascular imaging

investigation_details.investigation_name: Ankle-brachial index and vascular imaging (duplex/CT/MR angiography)
investigation_details.expected_result: ABI typically <0.4 with significant arterial stenoses/occlusions amenable to revascularisation
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The severity/level of disease is not defined and limb-saving revascularisation cannot be planned.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A severely reduced ABI with imaging-confirmed occlusive disease defines critical limb ischaemia.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Ischaemic rest pain relieved by dependency, evolving from claudication, with trophic changes and (sealed) absent pulses/positive Buerger's test, indicates critical limb ischaemia - a limb-threatening emergency needing urgent same-day vascular assessment and revascularisation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms and quantifies critical ischaemia; requires in-person assessment.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Vascular imaging (duplex / CT or MR angiography)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the level/extent of disease for revascularisation planning.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting lipids, HbA1c and cardiovascular risk assessment
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Given systemic atherosclerosis, assess and treat overall cardiovascular risk.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Routine outpatient referral with home opioid analgesia only
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Critical limb ischaemia is limb-threatening and needs urgent same-day vascular assessment, not a routine referral with painkillers alone.
